searching.label: Did you know?
quotes: humans can form a first impression in under 100 milliseconds
talking to strangers is proven to improve your mood
loneliness activates the same brain region as physical pain
eye contact increases feelings of connection even with strangers
the average person meets around 80,000 people in their lifetime
strangers are more likely to be honest with each other than friends
the human brain can recognize a familiar face in just 380 milliseconds
people who talk to strangers report higher daily happiness levels
your voice changes slightly depending on who you are talking to
humans are the only species that blushes
the average person spends 70% of their waking hours communicating
silence longer than 4 seconds in a conversation causes anxiety in both people
laughter is 30 times more likely to occur in social situations than alone
the average conversation lasts around 7 minutes before a natural pause
people unconsciously mirror the body language of whoever they talk to
hearing your own name activates a unique region of the brain
humans can detect over 10,000 different facial expressions
a genuine smile uses 17 muscles, a fake one uses 43
the feeling of being watched is so strong the brain has a dedicated neural circuit for it
people reveal more personal information in text than face to face
the brain releases oxytocin during any positive social interaction
humans are naturally wired to cooperate — it releases the same reward as food
the average person tells 1 to 2 lies per day without realizing it
two strangers can feel deeply connected after 36 specific questions
the sound of a human voice can reduce cortisol levels within seconds
